subclassFeature.name: College of Blades
entries: The College of Blades is among the flashiest and most theatrical of bard traditions — instead of the gentle entertainment of music or oratory, they opt for thrilling displays of swordsmanship and knifework. Blade bards may engage in any performance that seeks to awe and impress with gleaming weaponry, from knife throwing to sword swallowing to staged acrobatic duels.

Blade bards are not peerless warriors, not necessarily; rather, they are jugglers, entertainers, and daredevils whose chosen art demands ever-greater risks. They may come from any background, and are often fiercely independent, chaotic in their relationships with law and society even if they practice fierce internal discipline for their dangerous trade.

It is highly recommended to pair the College of Blades with {@link these Variant Two-Weapon Fighting rules|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-MpAZdyDDY7vb9Hzu14n}. Among other changes, these rules allow two-weapon fighting without using your bonus action.
Note that throughout, the College of Blades gets abilities related to attacking with melee weapons. It says nothing about making "melee weapon attacks"; i.e., thrown weapons work just fine.

entries.name: College of Blades Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: Rumor has it you played with a knife as a baby instead of a rattle.
Nobody has ever been able to find every {@item dagger|PHB} you keep hidden on your person.
You polish all your blades to a mirror finish, and then use them to style your hair.
When you're bored, you tend to juggle {@item dagger|PHB|daggers}, or challenge companions to sword-swallowing contests.
Your hands and arms are covered in scars from the effort of learning your trade.
Your skin is flawless, with nary a scar or scratch.

subclassFeature.name: Blade Master
entries: When you join the College of Blades at 3rd level, you gain the following benefits.

items: You can use a melee weapon you are holding as a spellcasting focus.
When you make a ranged attack with a weapon that has the thrown property, you can draw another weapon as part of the attack.
You gain proficiency with scimitars.
When you make a weapon attack using a weapon with the finesse property, you can use the average of your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers in place of your Dexterity modifier

entries.name: A Quick Example
entries: If your Dexterity is 14 (+2) and your Charisma is 18 (+4), you would have a +3 to attack and damage rolls. Remember to always round down.

subclassFeature.name: Blade Tricks
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn several techniques for overwhelming your opponents with skilled bladework. You can use each of these a number of times equal to the average of your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers, regaining all uses after completing a long rest. When you use a Blade Trick, you may choose to empower it by expending a spell slot. You can use each Blade Trick only once per round. When your Blade Tricks call for a saving throw, you may choose the DC to be either your spell save DC or calculated as 8 + your proficiency bonus + the average of your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers.

entries.name: Unseen Dagger
items: As a bonus action when you hit an enemy within 30 feet with an attack, you may make an attack with a melee weapon. If it is against a different target, you have advantage on the attack roll. If it hits, the attack does {@scaledamage 1d6|1-5|1d6} bonus damage. If you empower this trick with a spell slot, the damage increases by 1d6 per slot level, up to a maximum of 5d6 added damage for a 5th-level slot.

entries.name: Flashing Parry
items: As a reaction when an enemy would hit you with a melee attack while you are wielding a melee weapon, you may add your Charisma modifier to your AC until the end of the turn. If you empower this trick with a spell slot, the AC bonus increases by 1. It increases by 1 again if the slot was 3rd level or higher, and by 1 again if the slot was 5th level or higher.

entries.name: Fan of Knives
items: As an action, you may conjure a spray of blades which strike up to three creatures of your choice within 30 feet. Each target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@scaledamage 2d4|1-9|1d4} piercing damage on a failure or half as much on a success. If you empower this trick with a spell slot, the damage increases by 1d4 per slot level.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Flourish
entries: Also at 6th level, anytime you use a {@subclassFeature Blade Tricks|Bard|PHB|Blades|SMRBards|3|SMRBards|Blade Trick}, you may grant a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} as part of the same action.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Bladework
entries: Starting at 14th level, when you use a {@subclassFeature Blade Tricks|Bard|PHB|Blades|SMRBards|3|SMRBards|Blade Trick} while you have no uses of {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} left, you regain one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: College of Bravura
entries: The College of Bravura is for bards who are refined, elegant, and perhaps a touch arrogant. Some have received training in the swashbuckler's arts, while others naturally exude an easy confidence. But all bravura bards have decided to set aside a little of their own ego, in favor of lending it to their allies.

Bravura bards come from any walk of life; indeed, detractors may assume all bards start their careers in this college. But the College of Bravura requires a certain swagger, which tends to draw humans, particularly, to its practice.

entries.name: College of Bravura Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: When asked to perform, your first instinct is always to sing a sea shanty.
You always vault over objects when running, even if there isn't any real need to do so.
You consider it a personal failure anytime you're not the best-dressed in a room.
Your weapons are always polished to a mirror sheen.
You keep a treasure map in your boot pocket.
You bow to your audience after winning a fight.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you join the College of Bravura at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with firearms and {@item buckler|SMRBards|bucklers}, and one skill of your choice out of {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, and {@skill Persuasion}. If you are already proficient in all three, you gain proficiency in any skill of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Keen Eye
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can encourage your allies with a master swordsman's keen eye and deft strike. For 1 minute after an ally expends one of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice, their attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

Additionally, as a reaction after a creature within 30 feet scores a critical hit, you may expend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1}, rolling the die three times and adding the result to the damage dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Nimble Step
entries: Also at 3rd level, when you grant {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} to an ally, they may use their reaction to move 10 feet without provoking attacks of opportunity.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Turn the Tide
entries: Starting at 14th level, anytime an ally within 30 feet scores a critical hit, you recover one expended use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1}. In addition, once per turn when you miss with an attack on your turn, you may expend a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} and add the result to your attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, it becomes a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: College of the Canvas
entries: The College of the Canvas is for bards who channel their magic through art, painting reality to match their vision. A canvas bard sees the world not as it is, but as they wish it to be — and then makes that happen. Their artistic training and natural talent is able to tap into something fundamental about the world, and allow them to reshape it.

Canvas bards usually come from some degree of comfort — artistic skill rarely comes cheaply. They can sit anywhere on the normal axes of alignment, since imposing one's will on the world can be done in a chaotic or lawful fashion, and may be altruistic or profoundly selfish.

entries.name: College of the Canvas Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: All of your clothes are stained and paint-spattered.
Even your mundane paintings sometimes move, winking or growling at onlookers.
You have a bad habit of accidentally drawing a paintbrush instead of a weapon.
You insist on wearing a lopsided floppy hat.
You {@i hate} musicians.
You sometimes paint your companions without their permission.

subclassFeature.name: Artistic Genius
entries: When you join the College of the Canvas at 3rd level, you hone your skill with brush and ink. You gain proficiency with {@item painter's supplies|PHB}, or another tool of your choice if you're already proficient. Anytime you make a check to use your {@item painter's supplies|PHB}, you can add your Charisma modifier to the result.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Paints
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can paint a spell you know in a way that makes it usable by others. As an action, you can quickly begin to paint a change into the world. Select one creature that can see you within 60 feet and then expend a number of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice to select one of the effects below:

items: {@b 1+ dice:} The target gains or loses a number of hit points equal to one roll of all the {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice expended.
{@b 2 dice:} The target gains or loses one level of {@condition exhaustion}.
{@b 3 dice:} The target gains or loses the {@condition blinded}, {@condition grappled}, or {@condition poisoned} condition.
{@b 4 dice:} The target is {@condition charmed} by you or {@condition frightened} of you.
{@b 5 dice:} The target falls asleep, and can be awoken as an action or when it takes any damage.

entries: The effect occurs at the start of your next turn, and you must {@condition concentration||concentrate} on the painting (as if you were {@condition concentration||concentrating} on a spell). The target can attempt a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, preventing the effect on a success. For ongoing effects, an unwilling creature can reattempt the Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.

subclassFeature.name: Representational Rituals
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to paint your ideas into reality. You can cast a ritual by painting the spell onto a canvas or other flat surface. You can cast a bard spell you don't know in this way if you expend a number of Bardic Inspiration uses equal to the spell's level.

subclassFeature.name: Life Imitates Art
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can paint entire creatures into existence. When you finish a long rest, you can prepare a special set of pigments, suitable for casting one of the following spells: {@spell summon animal spirit|SpellsThatDontSuck}, {@spell summon archon|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell summon fell machine|SoManySpells}, {@spell summon grave spirit|SpellsThatDontSuck}, {@spell summon monstrosity|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell summon ooze|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell summon plant|KibblesTasty:CC}, and {@spell summon swarm|KibblesTasty:CC}.

You can spend 10 minutes painting to cast the chosen spell, which does not expend a spell slot. The spell casts at the highest level you're able to cast up to a maximum of 5th level. If you use your {@subclassFeature Potent Paints|Bard|PHB|Canvas|SMRBards|3} while {@condition concentration||concentrating} on this spell, you are able to maintain {@condition concentration} on both simultaneously, but when you lose {@condition concentration}, both the spell and {@subclassFeature Potent Paints|Bard|PHB|Canvas|SMRBards|3} end.
You can only have one set of special pigments prepared at a time, and they are consumed when you use them.

If you'd rather not look up a whole pile of new spells for this feature, you can instead allow any {@condition concentration} spell of 3rd level or lower that summons one creature with a scaling stat block.

subclassFeature.name: Art Imitates Life
entries: Also at 6th level, you can spend 1 minute to paint a nonmagical object into existence. The object must cost 10 gp or less, weigh 5 pounds or less, and be a nonmagical object you have previously seen. It disappears after 10 minutes or when it takes or deals any damage.

subclassFeature.name: Painter of Worlds
entries: Starting at 14th level, your art is able to fully reshape reality. You learn the {@spell wish} spell, which you can only cast by painting. You must spend 1 hour painting to cast the spell, which doesn't expend a spell slot. Once you cast it, you must finish a long rest before you can cast it again.

Initially, you can only use {@spell wish} to duplicate the effects of a spell of 7th level or lower. When you reach 15th level, you can use it to duplicate the effects of a spell of 8th level or lower. When you reach 17th level, there are no restrictions on how you can use it. If you cast {@spell wish} through this feature in a way that would cause you to permanently lose the ability to do so, you instead recover after 1 week.

subclassFeature.name: College of Myth
entries: The College of Myth is for bards who revere the old tales, who seek out lost legends and ancient stories and attempt to bring them back to life. Where other bards might sing songs or read poetry, a College of Myth bard would instead choose to recite a forgotten epic, weaving their words so skillfully the audience can picture the heroes contained therein. A myth bard's recitations are so powerful, in fact, they can imbue their allies with the spirit of those lost heroes — emboldening listeners to repeat mighty deeds that are only spoken of in legend.

Myth bards are rarely combatants themselves, save with a backup dagger or emergency crossbow. Their place is behind their allies, weaving stories while avoiding the fray themselves. Myth bards are somewhat more likely to come from wealthy or powerful backgrounds — high status helps afford years of research in dusty libraries, after all. But they can be of any alignment, as they seek to uncover old myths for whatever purpose fits their need.

entries.name: College of Myth Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: You cannot resist listening anytime you hear a story you don't know.
You insist on telling your comrades bedtime stories, even if they protest.
You maintain that ancient civilizations have lost wisdom, which must be rediscovered.
Your allies carry extra socks for stuffing in your mouth as needed.
You won a memorization contest once as a child, and just...never stopped.
You have a historical reference for every situation.

subclassFeature.name: Careful Historian
entries: When you join the College of Myth at 3rd level, you focus your skills on the deep and dedicated study of myths. You gain proficiency in the {@skill History} skill, or another skill of your choice if you are already proficient. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for all {@skill History} checks.

subclassFeature.name: Mythic Tales
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can inspire your allies to feats of legendary valor when you recite ancient myths. When you grant a creature a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} die, you can also endow them with the essence of a mythic hero. Instead of using the die to modify a roll, they can expend it as a bonus action to call upon the myth and gain a powerful benefit for 1 minute afterward. You know all of the following mythic tales, but can only prepare two each day. You may imbue each prepared tale into a creature once, regaining its use after a long rest. You can change your selections after a long rest.

entries.name: The Clever Fool
items: The clever fool is a hapless figure, tested beyond their limits but always succeeding despite themselves. When an ally calls upon the clever fool, they may treat any result of 1 on the {@dice d20} as a 20 instead when rolling ability checks, attack rolls, or saving throws. As a reaction after they miss with an attack or fail a saving throw, they may choose to end this effect in order to succeed at the roll.

entries.name: The Indomitable Warrior
items: The indomitable warrior is a mighty champion, undefeated in combat despite facing terrible odds. When an ally calls upon the indomitable warrior, they gain temporary hit points equal to half your proficiency bonus after every time they take damage. Attacks against them are made at disadvantage while they have temporary hit points from this effect. When they are reduced to 0 hit points, they may choose to end this effect to instead drop to 1 hit point and immediately make one attack.

entries.name: The Cursed King
items: The cursed king is a tragic figure, doomed to fail despite (or sometimes because of) his good intentions. When an ally calls upon the cursed king, they select a creature within 30 feet to share the king's fate. The target has disadvantage on all ability checks and attack rolls, and takes psychic damage equal to your proficiency bonus every time it misses with an attack. The target can make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.

As a reaction after the target misses with an attack, the ally may choose to end the effect to reveal the target's fate. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition incapacitated} by despair until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: The Peerless Huntress
items: The peerless huntress is an iconic warrior, wandering the forests and tracking game with an unerring eye. When an ally calls upon the peerless huntress, they may select any creature within 60 feet as their quarry. Their ranged attacks against their quarry may ignore {@quickref cover|PHB|3|0|half and three-quarters cover}, and have their normal range doubled. As a reaction when the enemy leaves their sight, they may choose to end the effect to immediately move up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

entries.name: The Savage Beast
items: The savage beast is a fearsome brute, a terrifying creature that stalks the wilderness. When an ally calls upon the savage beast, their jump distance is doubled and their speed increases by 15 feet. As a reaction after they move 20 feet and hit with a melee attack, they may choose to end this effect to deal {@damage 2d8||The Savage Beast} bonus damage. This damage increases by 1d8 when your proficiency bonus increases.

entries.name: The Pious Lord
items: The pious lord is a noble hero, faithful to the gods and heedful of all obligations. When an ally calls upon the pious lord, they gain {@dice 1d4||The Pious Lord} temporary hit points at the start of each of their turns. As a reaction after an ally fails an attack roll or saving throw, they may choose to end this effect to give that ally a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} and immediately add it to the result, potentially changing the outcome.

subclassFeature.name: Teller of Tales
entries: Starting at 6th level, your mythic recitations embolden you as well as your audience. When an ally within 30 feet calls upon one of your {@subclassFeature Mythic Tales|Bard|PHB|Myth|SMRBards|3}, you immediately receive temporary hit points equal to your bard level, and recover one spell slot of a level equal to or lower than your proficiency bonus. These temporary hit points last until the ally is no longer inspired by your tale.

subclassFeature.name: Mythic Might
entries: Starting at 14th level, your tale-telling is nearly as legendary as the tales themselves. You gain the following benefits:

items: You may prepare three {@subclassFeature Mythic Tales|Bard|PHB|Myth|SMRBards|3} each day instead of two.

When an ally calls upon one of your {@subclassFeature Mythic Tales|Bard|PHB|Myth|SMRBards|3}, all creatures of their choice within 15 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or become either {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} of the ally (ally's choice) until the end of their next round.

When a non-hostile creature starts its turn within 5 feet of a creature benefiting from your {@subclassFeature Mythic Tales|Bard|PHB|Myth|SMRBards|3}, it gains advantage on the first attack it makes before the end of its turn.

subclassFeature.name: College of Pyrotechnics
entries: The College of Pyrotechnics is for bards whose art is less about song and dance than alchemistry — bards driven to create dazzling displays of fireworks. They construct impressive rockets, able to explode with brilliant colors and thundering sound. They often find employment serving celebrations and parades, but are equally likely to be pressed into military service when their art if required.

Pyrotechnics bards are astounding entertainers, able to delight and amaze with their curious arts. They use magic to augment their fireworks, producing colors and shapes impossible with chemistry alone. Virtually all pyrotechnics bards have backgrounds involving apprenticeship or study, enabling them to learn their rarified art. They tend towards good and neutral alignments; black-hearted villains usually find more direct routes to power than entertainment displays.

entries.name: College of Pyrotechnics Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: You like to dress in bright and shimmering colors, like your fireworks.
Your fingertips have permanent scorch marks.
You don't have any eyebrows.
In dark places, you prefer carrying sparklers to torches.
You jealously guard the secrets of your craft.
You always smell of gunpowder.

subclassFeature.name: Expert Illuminator
entries: When you join the College of Pyrotechnics at 3rd level, you learn the basics of alchemical ignition and pyrotechnic displays. You gain proficiency with {@item alchemist's supplies|PHB} or another set of tools if you are already proficient. When you make ability checks to craft bombs or fireworks, you do so with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Delightful Sparklers
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can produce small fireworks for others to use. A creature holding one of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice can use it to throw one of these small explosives as a bonus action. When it does so, one creature within 15 feet of it makes its next attack roll before the end of its next turn with disadvantage. If the attack still hits, its damage is reduced by one roll of the die.
Additionally, while a creature holds one of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice, it can choose to emit {@quickref Vision and Light||2||bright light} in a 10-foot radius.

subclassFeature.name: Fireworks Displays
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn how to produce stunning displays of fireworks, amazing your enemies and stunning your foes. As an action, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} to perform any of your Fireworks Displays. If the Display calls for a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC. When you perform a display, you can enhance its effects by expending a spell slot of any level.

entries.name: Dazzling Display
items: You fire a rocket at a point you can see within 120 feet. The rocket explodes in a brilliant display of color and smoke, forcing each creature within 30 feet to succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Enhancement
entries: If you expend a spell slot, creatures failing the saving throw take {@scaledamage 1d12|1-9|1d12} radiant damage for each slot level.

entries.name: Inspiring Display
items: You fire a series of rockets up into the sky, impressing onlookers. Up to four creatures of your choice that can see the display clearly are heartened by it, gaining temporary hit points equal to one roll of your Bardic Inspiration die.

items.name: Enhancement
entries: If you expend a spell slot, the temporary hit points are increased by the slot level, and you can choose two more creatures for each slot level.

subclassFeature.name: Intentional Misfire
entries: At 6th level, you learn to create a new type of {@subclassFeature Fireworks Displays|Bard|PHB|Pyrotechnics|SMRBards|3|SMRBards|Fireworks Display}.

entries.name: Repurposed Rocket
items: You launch a damaged rocket which emits a shower of sparks along its path before exploding in a blast of light and sound. As an action, you can launch this rocket at a point you can see within 120 feet. The rocket throws sparks in a 5-foot-wide line as it travels, causing every creature in its path to succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take fire damage equal to half your bard level.

When the rocket reaches its destination, it explodes, causing every creature within 30 feet to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 4d4||Repurposed Rocket} thunder damage and is {@condition deafened} and staggered (reducing its speed by half) until the end of its next turn; on a success, it takes half as much damage and suffers no other effects.

items.name: Enhancement
entries: If you expend a spell slot, every creature that fails either saving throw against the rocket's effects takes {@scaledamage 2d4|1-9|2d4} fire damage for each slot level.

subclassFeature.name: Emergency Flare
entries: Also at 6th level, you can throw a {@subclassFeature Delightful Sparklers|Bard|PHB|Pyrotechnics|SMRBards|3|SMRBards|Delightful Sparkler} as a reaction before a creature attacks you.

subclassFeature.name: All-Fire
entries: At 14th level, you devise a method for releasing massive quantities of fireworks, all at once. As an action, you can expend any number of {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice. For each die you expend, you perform one of your {@subclassFeature Fireworks Displays|Bard|PHB|Pyrotechnics|SMRBards|3}, all simultaneously. Every creature that fails any saving throw takes additional radiant damage equal to one roll of all the {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} dice spent. No creature can be affected by the same type of display more than once. When performed in this way, you can only enhance one display by expending a spell slot.
You can perform an All-Fire once, regaining its use after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: College of the Quiver
entries: The College of the Quiver is a bardic order dedicated to the hunt, practiced with ranged weaponry and oriented around displays of martial skill. They are still performers, but with the bow as their brush and a hunting quarry as a canvas. Quiver bards practice daily with bow, crossbow, or firearm, but their mastery of weapons is limited, and focused on skill for its own sake. They sometimes make fearsome warriors, but just as often prefer contests or target shooting to the battlefield.

Quiver bards can be of any alignment and background. Some are flashy chevaliers, exercising their skills for the good of all. Others are selfish showmen, intent only on dominating the competition. And while some quiver bards idly-rich nobles practicing a sporting hobby, others may work their way up from humble origins, as trackers, soldiers, or hunters.

entries.name: College of the Quiver Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: You run at your fastest when on a creature's trail.
You mark a notch on your bow for every kill.
Your back is covered with tattoos of fearsome prey you've downed.
You like to use a crossbow bolt as an eating utensil.
Your fingers are gnarled and calloused from the bowstring.
You sing an atonal hymn as you loose your arrow.

subclassFeature.name: Thrill of the Hunt
entries: When you join the College of the Quiver at 3rd level, you take on a fierce love of the hunt. You gain proficiency in either {@skill Survival} or {@skill Perception}, or another skill of your choice if you're already proficient in both. You also gain proficiency with {@filter all martial ranged weapons|items|source=|category=|type=martial weapon;ranged weapon=sand}, as well as {@filter all martial weapons with the thrown property|items|source=|category=|type=martial weapon|property=thrown}.

Additionally, as a bonus action when you make a Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) check to attempt to follow a creature's recent trail, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1}, rolling your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} die and adding the result.

subclassFeature.name: Arrowsong
entries: Also at 3rd level, you hone your mastery with ranged weapons to a remarkable degree, learning a number of special techniques, known as arrowsongs. Once per turn on your turn when you make a ranged weapon attack, you can choose to use one of these special techniques.

items.name: Song of Seeking
entries: Your projectile homes in on its foe. Your attack is made at advantage and ignores {@quickref cover|PHB|3|0}.

items.name: Song of the Heart-Seeker
entries: Your projectile is aimed directly for the target's weak point. Your attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

items.name: Song of Thunder
entries: Your projectile carries a wave of thunder as it slams into the target. On a hit, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 3d6} thunder damage and fall {@condition prone}.

You can use each of these techniques once, regaining its use after finishing a short or long rest. You can also expend a spell slot when you use a technique to increase its power. If you do so, the attack deals {@scaledamage 1d6|1-5|1d6} additional force damage per slot level, up to 5d6 at 5th level.

subclassFeature.name: Ranged Weapon Expert
entries: Also from 3rd level, you can make ranged attacks with tremendous swiftness. When you make a ranged attack through your subclass features, you can ignore the loading property. If the attack is with a thrown weapon, you can draw an additional thrown weapon as part of the attack.

subclassFeature.name: Swift Shot
entries: At 6th level, your skill with ranged attacks is almost automatic. When you grant {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} to a creature as a bonus action, you can make one ranged attack with the same bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Merciless Hunt
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can enter a fearsome focus state, tearing into your enemies with a barrage of ranged attacks. As an action, you can expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to immediately make two ranged attacks against a single target. For each two slot levels above 3rd, you can make one additional attack.

subclassFeature.name: Sure Shot
entries: Additionally at 14th level, your ranged attacks take on a deadly precision. The first time each turn you hit a target with a ranged attack, you can add your Charisma modifier to the damage dealt.

baseitem.name: Buckler
entries: A buckler is a small shield, typically made of metal, which is held in the fist rather than strapped to the arm. A buckler only grants a bonus of +1 to its wielder's armor class, but can be donned or doffed with a free object interaction.
Bucklers function as shields for purposes of feats and other rules concerning shields, and any character proficient with shields is also proficient with bucklers.
